entry.title: New Sweet Chili Chicken Strips Arrive at Dairy Queen
entry.summary:
Dairy Queen launches new Sweet Chili Chicken Strips as a new flavor for its Sauced & Tossed Chicken Strips Baskets.
The Sweet Chili sauce is a limited-time option that the chain describes as a "combination of tangy sweetness and a subtle kick of heat." Sweet chili sauce is a Thai sauce made with red chili peppers that is sweet, spicy, and slightly tangy. The Sweet Chili Sauced & Tossed Chicken Strips Basket features four or six chicken strips tossed in new Sweet Chili sauce, served with fries, Texas toast, and a choice of dipping sauce. Dairy Queen also offers Honey BBQ as a Sauced & Tossed flavor for its chicken strips. Photo via Dairy Queen. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Starbucks Welcomes New Protein Cold Foam and Protein Lattes
entry.summary:
Starbucks introduces new Protein Cold Foam drinks and Protein Lattes, featuring up to 36 grams of protein in a grande-sized beverage. The extra protein comes from whey protein isolate.
Starbucks Protein Cold Foam (pictured) can be added to any cold coffee, tea, or Refreshers, and adds 15 grams of protein to a grande. It costs $2 extra (prices may vary) and comes in several flavors, including new banana, vanilla, chocolate, matcha, salted caramel brown sugar, plain, sugar-free vanilla, pumpkin (seasonal), and pecan (seasonal). Starbucks Protein-Boosted Milk, made by blending 2% milk with whey protein isolate, can be substituted for regular milk in any drink that contains milk for $1 more (prices may vary). In addition, the coffeehouse chain adds several Protein Cold Foam beverages to the menu, including the new Iced Banana Cream Protein Matcha, Iced Vanilla Cream Protein Latte, and Chocolate Cream Protein Cold Brew. They're joined by new Protein Lattes, including new Sugar-Free Vanilla Protein Matcha, Sugar-Free Vanilla Protein Latte, Protein Matcha, and Vanilla Protein Matcha. The lattes are available iced or hot, while the Cold Foam drinks are only available iced. Both Protein Cold Foam and Protein Lattes are available now in the U.S. and Canada. Photo via Starbucks. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Chick-fil-A Releases New Waffle Potato Chips in Grocery Stores
entry.summary:
Chick-fil-A expands its retail lineup with the test launch of new Waffle Potato Chips at select grocery stores in 12 states in the Southeast and Atlantic regions starting this month.
The release follows the restaurant debut of Waffle Potato Chips earlier this year. Chick-fil-A Waffle Potato Chips come in two varieties: Chick-fil-A Sauce Flavored Waffle Potato Chips and Original Flavor Waffle Potato Chips. The former offers the smoky, tangy, and slightly sweet taste of Chick-fil-A's signature sauce, while the latter features classic waffle-cut potato chips seasoned with sea salt. Both varieties are available now in 7-oz bags at select Publix, Kroger, and Walmart stores in Georgia, Florida, North Carolina, South Carolina, Alabama, Tennessee, Virginia, West Virginia, Kentucky, Ohio, Arkansas, and Missouri. The chips join a Chick-fil-A retail lineup that includes sauces and dressings. Photo via Chick-fil-A. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Review: Wendy's Chicken Tenders
entry.summary:
Wendy's Chicken Tenders feature chicken breast tenders with a seasoned, crispy-fried breading.
Three tenders cost me $5.49. Interestingly, three McDonald's McCrispy Strips cost $6.39 in my area, but four McCrispy Strips are $7.39, while four Wendy's Tenders are $7.49. It'll be interesting to see if Wendy's raises the price on three Tenders after the initial launch period. I don't know if this is standard practice for the new Tenders or if it's just something my local Wendy's does, but I was told when ordering that it would take six minutes as the Tenders are cooked to order. I did go late at night, though. As a result, my Chicken Tenders were piping hot. They were satisfyingly crispy on the surface with a moderate amount of blooming-type batter. Inside, they were juicy and tender with a m…

entry.title: Arby's Serves Up New Steak Nuggets
entry.summary:
Embracing its "we have the meats" slogan, Arby's launches new Steak Nuggets, which are seasoned bite-sized pieces of steak in a similar vein as Chick-fil-A's Grilled Nuggets.
Specifically, Arby's Steak Nuggets are hand-cut, seasoned with garlic and pepper, and then seared and smoked. They're meant to be convenient to eat on the go with your hands or a fork. Steak Nuggets are available on their own, in a sandwich, or in a bowl. You can get five or nine Steak Nuggets a la carte with a side of Hickory BBQ Sauce. The Steak Nugget Sandwich features Steak Nuggets with Havarti cheese, crispy-fried onions, pickles, and mayo on a toasted bun. The Steak Nugget Bowl features Steak Nuggets and crispy-fried onions on white cheddar Mac ‘n Cheese. Steak Nuggets are available now for a limited time at Arby’s restaurants nationwide. Photo via Arby's. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Capriotti's Introduces New Chicken Bacon Ranch Sandwich
entry.summary:
Capriotti's launches the new Chicken Bacon Ranch sandwich as a response to customer demand for "bold flavors and heartier portions."
The new sandwich features 25% more thinly sliced grilled chicken, hickory-smoked bacon, melted white American cheese, lettuce, tomato, and ranch on a soft roll. Prices vary, but here in the Los Angeles area, the Chicken Bacon Ranch costs $8.49 for a mini-sized sandwich, $12.99 for a small, $14.99 for a medium, and $24.99 for a large. Additionally, Capriotti's is running a promotion this month where its rewards members can get $5 off an order of $30 or more using the code "CBR25" when ordering online. Photo via Capriotti's. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Taco John's Welcomes New Spicy Beef & Jalapeno Burrito and Crispy Chicken Bacon Ranch Burrito
entry.summary:
Taco John's introduces two new burritos to the menu: the Spicy Beef & Jalapeno Burrito and the Crispy Chicken Bacon Ranch Burrito.
The Spicy Beef & Jalapeno Burrito features seasoned ground beef, Potato Oles, nacho cheese, lettuce, tomato, sour cream, pickled jalapenos, and Super Hot Sauce, wrapped in a warm flour tortilla. Taco John's Crispy Chicken Bacon Ranch Burrito features Crispy Chicken Tenders, Potato Oles, nacho cheese, lettuce, tomato, bacon, and new Marzetti Ranch, wrapped in a warm flour tortilla. Both burritos are available a la carte or in a combo with Potato Oles and a fountain drink. Also, starting October 21, 2025, Taco John's will be bringing back its Apple Grande. The dessert is a crispy cinnamon-sugar tortilla topped with warm apple filling, cinnamon candy spice, and Cheddar cheese topping. Photo via Taco John's. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Applebee's Debuts New Grilled Cheese Cheeseburger and Pickle Fries
entry.summary:
Applebee's launches the new Grilled Cheese Cheeseburger and Crispy Pickle Fries this fall. Joining the new dishes are this year's round of Halloween-themed Spooky Sips.
The new Grilled Cheese Cheeseburger arrives as a new entree option for the casual dining chain's 2 for $25 deal. It features a grilled beef patty, four slices of American cheese, and a sweet and savory jam, served on toasted potato bread with tomato basil soup on the side for dipping. Applebee's 2 for $25 deal is a long-running deal that lets you choose two entrees from a limited selection and one appetizer or two side salads for $25 at participating locations. It is available for dine-in, takeout, or delivery. New Crispy Pickle Fries join the available options for Applebee's Ultimate Trio sampler. The new side features crispy-fried pickle fries tossed in Tajin chile-lime seasoning, served with Mexi-ranch sauce for dipping. The Ultimate Trio was launched earlier this year, ahead of the football season, and is a build-your-own sampler special. You choose three appetizers and three dipping sauces for $14.99 at participating locations. As with the 2 for $25 deal, it's available for dine-in, takeout, and delivery. Applebee's limited-time Spooky Sips cocktails for the 2025 Halloween season introduce new $2 Vibe Drops that add an additional layer of flavor and a color-changing effect. This year's lineup includes: - New Tipsy Zombie - A mix of Bacardi Superior Rum, passion fruit, pineapple, cherry, and lime, topped with a gummi brain. You can add the $2 Melon Liqueur Vibe Drop to change it from blue to green. - New Dracula's Juice - Bacardi Superior Rum and Patrón Silver Tequila, shaken with strawberry and premium lemon sour. You can add the $2 Blue Curaçao Vibe Drop to change it from red to purple. Photo via Applebee's. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Panda Express Launches New Crispy Sesame Shrimp & Beef
entry.summary:
Panda Express serves up new Crispy Sesame Shrimp & Beef as its latest seasonal entree starting October 8, 2025.
The new offering features tempura-battered shrimp, crispy-fried beef strips, green beans, and red bell peppers wok-tossed with a "savory, subtly spicy umami sauce." The shrimp is the same as from Panda Express' Honey Walnut Shrimp, while the beef comes from the chain's Beijing Beef. According to Panda Express, Crispy Sesame Shrimp & Beef is "inspired by the classic Sichuan dish, Mouthwatering Chicken," which is poached chicken served cold with a chili oil sauce. Also on October 8, Panda Express will be introducing its Wok Wednesdays promotion (as a play on Taco Tuesdays). Each Wednesday, the promotion offers a game that can be played through the Panda Express mobile app to unlock "reward offers" for use on the same day. Photo via Panda Express. Read more at Brand Eating!
